{"body":"Please provide as much information as you can:

* **GO term ID and Label**

GO:0051082  unfolded protein binding

* **Reason for deprecation** Put an x in the appropriate box: 

 - [ ]  The reason for obsoletion is that more specific terms were created.

The reason is that the binding term  should be annotated to an activity

new term "holdase chaperone" https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30552 >> unfolded protein carrier activity
OR possibly
GO:0044183 protein folding chaperone aka foldase 


* **Are there annotations to this term?**
- How many EXP: 194


* **Are there mappings and cross references to this term? (InterPro, Keywords; check QuickGO cross-references section)**

* **Is this term in a subset? (check the AmiGO page for that term)**

* **Any other information**


----
Checklist for ontology editor

***Check term usage and metadata in Protégé***
- [ ] check term usage in the ontology 
- [ ] check internal mappings: RHEA, EC, MetaCyc
- [ ] check subset usage
- [ ] check taxon constraints 

***Check annotations***
- [ ] create Annotation Review spreadsheet 
- * Annotation Review template: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1izV3q1jnq_Ybg5MJ53O_b5BCTkGtjl_Fi-cOg3-7eBE/edit#gid=0
- * Instructions: https://wiki.geneontology.org/Annotation_Review_Request
- [ ] create annotation review ticket: https://github.com/geneontology/go-annotation/issues/new?assignees=&labels=annotation+review&template=annotation-review-for-obsoletion.md&title=Review+annotations+to+%5BINSERT+GO+TERM%5D
- [ ] find external mappings (via IEAS), include in obsoletion notice  
- [ ] tag curators in Annotation Review ticket. Responsible annotator for each group: https://github.com/geneontology/go-site/blob/master/metadata/group-contacts.csv

***Notification***
- [ ] create [obsoletion announcement](https://github.com/geneontology/go-announcements/issues/new?assignees=&labels=obsoletion&template=obsoletion-notice.md&title=Obsoletion+notice%3A+%5BGO+ID%3A+term+label%5D)
- [ ] announce to GO friends (go-friends@mailman.stanford.edu)
- [ ] paste the text in the ontology ticket

https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/20873","createdAt":"2025-10-30T10:01:47Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30962#issuecomment-3467090902","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7P7qnf","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Which terms are the proper replacements?","createdAt":"2025-11-05T00:17:09Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30962#issuecomment-3488524767","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7QEAcM","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"denatured protein binding
https://www.ebi.ac.uk/QuickGO/annotations?goUsage=descendants&goUsageRelationships=is_a,part_of,occurs_in&goId=GO:0031249&evidenceCode=ECO:0000269&evidenceCodeUsage=descendants

has only 5 annotations to 4 proteins.

HSPA1A
This seems to be a "non-carrier type holdase"  so we should probably add this parent term now
The broad family of LEA proteins are intrinsically disordered proteins (IDPs) with several potential roles in desiccation tolerance, or anhydrobiosis, one of which is to limit desiccation-induced aggregation of cellular proteins. We show here that this activity, termed molecular shield function, is distinct from that of a classical molecular chaperone, such as HSP70 -

HSPH1 (heat shock protein family H (Hsp110) member 1)
"Hsp110 protects heat-denatured proteins"
Also an non-carrier holdase 

SAN1 Here we show that the yeast nuclear PQC ubiquitin ligase San1 directly recognizes its misfolded substrates via intrinsically disordered N- and C-terminal domains. These disordered domains are punctuated with small segments of order and high sequence conservation that serve as substrate-recognition sites San1 uses to target its different substrates. We propose that these substrate-recognition sites, interspersed among flexible, disordered regions, provide San1 an inherent plasticity which allows it to bind its many, differently shaped misfolded substrates.

this is a Ub ligase detecting misfolded proteins, so in this case it appears to be acting as a "misfolded protein sensor" rather than a type of chaperone?  We don't have a term for this.
Maybe this should be a new term?

Fbxo2 also appears to be a sensor  "we propose that Fbs proteins distinguish native from unfolded glycoproteins by sensing the exposed chitobiose structure".

@pgaudet thoughts?

--

Some genes can have both MFs. PMID: 35398094 "Hsp90, Hsp70, and other molecular chaperones regulate misfolded protein accumulation through a combination of holdase, foldase, and disaggregase activities."

","createdAt":"2026-02-05T01:31:58Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30962#issuecomment-3850586973","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7m9fQi","author":{"login":"cmungall"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"AI-assisted review of human genes annotated to GO:0051082 below:                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                                            
  I reviewed all **33 human genes** with experimental annotations to GO:0051082 "unfolded protein binding", classifying each by mechanism to determine appropriate replacement terms. Full details and   
  per-gene review YAMLs: **[project page](https://ai4curation.io/ai-gene-review/pages/projects/UNFOLDED_PROTEIN_BINDING.html)**                                                                             
                                                                                                                                                                                                          
  - **MODIFY → GO:0044183** protein folding chaperone (22 genes): HSP70s, DnaJs, prefoldin, sHSPs, CLU, SCG5, GRPEL1
  - **MODIFY → GO:0140545** ATP-dependent protein disaggregase (3 genes): HSPA1A, HSPA1B, HSPA8 (also have GO:0044183)
  - **MODIFY → gene-specific MF** (2 genes): NPM1 → GO:0140713 histone chaperone; AIP → GO:0051879 Hsp90 protein binding
  - **MARK_AS_OVER_ANNOTATED** (5 genes, not chaperones): UGGT1, TOMM20, AHSA1, PTGES3, TMEM67
  - **REMOVE** (2 genes, sensors not chaperones): SYVN1, ERLEC1

  **Decisions needed from ontology editors:**
  - [ ] Create "holdase chaperone activity" (#30552) — 6 genes (CRYAA, CRYAB, HSPB6, DNAJB6, DNAJB8, CLU) are using GO:0044183 as a placeholder but are holdases, not foldases
  - [ ] Confirm GO:0044183 is acceptable as interim annotation for holdases
  - [ ] Consider whether "misfolded protein sensor activity" warrants a new term (for SYVN1-type E3 ligases)

  <details>
  <summary>Terminology used in this review</summary>

  - **Foldase**: ATP-dependent, actively assists protein refolding (e.g. HSP70)
  - **Holdase**: ATP-independent, binds unfolded proteins to prevent aggregation without refolding (e.g. alpha-crystallins)
  - **Disaggregase**: ATP-dependent solubilization of existing aggregates (HSP70 + DnaJ + Hsp110)
  - **Sensor**: Recognizes misfolded proteins as substrates for degradation, not chaperoning (e.g. SYVN1/HRD1 E3 ligase, UGGT1 glycoprotein quality sensor)

  </details>","createdAt":"2026-02-10T01:55:55Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/30962#issuecomment-3874878498","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws7nCpWm","author":{"login":"ValWood"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"

Useful.

A few comments.

1.  DNAJ proteins aren't foldases; they are co-chaperones (as substrate adaptors and activators of hsp70 folding chaperones). 

2. Note that the remapping will also depend on experimental context; some proteins are stictly holdases (small Tims), some  are strictly foldases, GroEL/ES, but some function as both holdases and foldases depending on context and conditions. For example hsp70, this can vary for conditions, clients, de novo or quality control etc 
Curators will need to review their annotations.

3. We  already have a term for "holdase" and should not use GO:0044183 
but we should  consider using the biologist-friendly labels:
foldase  instead of "protein folding chaperone" (removes the BP from the MF term)
and
holdase instead of  "GO:0140309 unfolded protein carrier activity" (we put holdase as broad, but I don't remember why)

4.  Refine "holdase" def
From: A protein carrier activity that binds to a protein in an unfolded state and escorts it between two different cellular components. The unfolded protein carrier prevents aggregation of the target protein.
 to include the aggregation before the period.
Suggest: A protein carrier activity that binds to an unfolded protein to prevent aggregation and escorts it between two different cellular components. 

5. "misfolded protein sensor" would be useful for the ubiquitin degradation pathways.

6. PTGES3 is a hsp90 co-chaperone (but I don't know what sort yet, could be activator, or substrate adapto or both) AHSA1 is a hsp90 activator.
